specification.topic-statement: The model selection command updates the active agent's LLM model, inferred provider, and thinking level with validation and database persistence.

scope.in-scope: Parsing the model/thinking-level syntax, inferring the provider from the model name, validating thinking level compatibility for Google models, updating in-memory agent state, invalidating the provider instance and token cache, persisting changes to the database, and displaying a confirmation with provider-specific thinking details. Also includes the /system command for setting the global system message.
scope.boundary: The mapping from model name to provider name is performed by an external function. This topic receives either the provider name or an error.
Thinking level compatibility checks and budget/level lookups for Google models are performed by external functions. This topic invokes them and handles their results.
Cache invalidation and re-pruning with the new provider's token counters are performed by external operations. This topic calls them in sequence.
The agents table is updated via an external helper. Errors are logged and do not block the command from completing.

field.name: thinking_level
field.note: appended after '/' separator; one of: min, low, med, high

field.name: thinking_level
field.note: stored as string: min, low, med, or high

model-command.argument-validation: If no argument is provided, an error is returned. If the agent is currently in WAITING_FOR_LLM state, an error is returned (model cannot be switched mid-request).
model-command.syntax-parsing: The argument is parsed for an optional '/' separator: - If no '/' is present: the entire argument is the model name; thinking level is absent (will use current). - If '/' is present with nothing after it: an error is returned ("trailing '/' with no thinking level"). - If '/' is present with an empty model name (slash at position 0): an error is returned ("empty model name"). - Otherwise: the portion before '/' is the model name and the portion after is the thinking level string.
model-command.provider-inference: The model name is mapped to a provider name. Models beginning with "gpt-", "o1", "o3", or "o4" map to "openai"; models beginning with "claude-" map to "anthropic"; models beginning with "gemini-" map to "google". Unrecognized models cause an error.
model-command.thinking-level-resolution: If a thinking level string was provided in the argument, it is converted to the internal enum value ("min" → MIN, "low" → LOW, "med" → MED, "high" → HIGH). An unrecognized string causes an error. If no thinking level was provided, the agent's current thinking level is used unchanged.
model-command.google-model-validation: For Google models only: the thinking level is validated against the model's capabilities. If the level is unsupported, an error is returned. For Gemini 2.5 models specifically, the model must also appear in the thinking budget lookup table; if it does not, an error is returned.
model-command.in-memory-state-update: The agent's previous provider string and model string are freed and replaced with newly allocated copies of the inferred provider and parsed model name. The agent's thinking level field is updated to the resolved enum value.
model-command.cache-invalidation: The agent's provider instance is invalidated so the next request creates a fresh instance with the new model. The token cache is fully invalidated and immediately re-pruned using the new provider's token counters.
model-command.database-persistence: If a database context is present, the agents table is updated with the new provider, model, and thinking level (stored as a string). If the update fails, the error is logged via JSONL and freed; the in-memory state remains authoritative.
model-command.user-feedback:
A confirmation message is appended to the scrollback. The message includes the provider name, model name, and thinking level details:
- Anthropic models with adaptive thinking: shows the effort string ("low", "medium", or "high"). - Anthropic models with budget thinking: shows the token budget with a "K" suffix for values over 1024. - Google Gemini 2.5 models: shows the token budget. - Google Gemini 3 models: shows the level string ("minimal", "low", "medium", or "high"). - OpenAI models: shows the effort string ("low", "medium", or "high").

behavior.system-command:
The /system command sets or clears the global system message.
If no argument is provided, the current system message is freed and the field is set to null. A "System message cleared" confirmation is appended to the scrollback.
If an argument is provided, the current system message is freed and replaced with a copy of the argument string. A "System message set to: …" confirmation is appended to the scrollback.
The system message is stored in the global configuration object, not on the agent. It affects all future requests regardless of which agent submits them.
The system message is not persisted to the database and is lost when the process restarts.
